sections.paragraph:
μόδιος, ίου, ὁ (Lat. loanw. [modius]: Dinarchus 1, 43 [?]; Dionys. Hal. 12, 1; Epict. 1, 17, 7; 9; Plut., Demetr. 33, 6; ins [OGI index]; PThéad 32, 25; PGen 62, 17; GrBar 6:7; Jos., Ant. 9, 85; 14, 28; 206. Loanw. in rabb.) a grain measure containing 16 sextarii = about 8.75 liters, almost one peck, a peck measure Mt 5:15; Mk 4:21; Lk 11:33 (a vessel used to hide a light, as Judg 7:16 [ὑδρίας]; Jos., Ant 5, 223.—On the figure, ADupont-Sommer, Note archéol. sur le prov. évang.: mettre la lampe sous le boisseau = Mél. Syr. à MRDussaud II ’39, 789–94; JJeremias, ZNW 39, ’41, 237–40 Pauly-W. III 1775f; Hultsch, Metrologie2 121ff).—M-M.

Lat. Lat. = Latin

loanw. loanw. = loanword

Dinarchus Dinarchus , orator, IV–III b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

PGen PGen = Les Papyrus de Genève—List 4

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

prov. prov. = proverbial(ly)

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 656
